summary: Mutation testing results: 76 killed, 5 survived, 1 no coverage. creation.ts scores 100% (9/9 killed). api-client.ts scores 91.78% (67/9 killed, 5 survived, 1 no-coverage).
testQuality: The creation tools themselves have excellent test coverage with 100% mutation score. The sdk/api-client.ts has 5 surviving mutants concentrated in the hasErrorField type guard (line 67) and the non-JSON error path (line 92-93). Tests do not verify the type guard against null values, primitives, or arrays, and do not assert that a successful plain-text 200 response is returned without throwing.

suggestions.description: 4 mutants survived in hasErrorField: logical operator changed from && to ||, and each sub-expression replaced with true. The guard is not tested against null, primitives, or arrays as error response bodies.
suggestions.rationale: Tests only call hasErrorField indirectly via error responses containing objects with or without an error field. No test passes null, a number, an array, or a non-null non-object, so mutations that skip the null or typeof checks are not caught.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: when the API returns a 500 with a JSON null body (fetchSpy returns jsonResponse(null, 500)), expect the client to throw with the fallback message 500: Request failed. Add another test with a JSON array body (["err"], 500) expecting the same fallback. These force hasErrorField to handle non-object values.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: if (!response.ok) changed to if (true) in the catch branch (non-JSON parse path). When a 200 response with non-JSON body is returned, the code should return the text, but no test verifies this.
suggestions.rationale: The existing non-JSON test only covers 502 status. Changing !response.ok to true would cause a 200 plain-text response to throw instead of returning the text, but no test catches this.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: when a GET endpoint returns a 200 plain-text response (textResponse(some-text, 200)), expect the resolved value to equal some-text rather than throwing. This kills the if(true) mutation.

suggestions.description: NoCoverage: the fallback string Request failed in text || Request failed (non-JSON error path) is never exercised. No test sends a non-JSON error response with an empty body.
suggestions.rationale: The existing non-JSON test sends a non-empty HTML body. Stryker reports this as NoCoverage because no test exercises the branch where text is empty.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: when the server returns a 503 with an empty body (textResponse("", 503)), expect the client to throw with 503: Request failed. This covers the empty-text fallback branch.
